name: llm-config
summary: Builds the clean LLM configuration object that gets written to field_llm_config. This is the contract every other GoUltra AI app depends on — they read it from a .llm-config-data div Drupal renders into the page. Inclusion rule: a provider is included only if enabled && key_verified && api_key, AND it has at least one model with active=true. A model is included only if active=true. Sensitive working fields are stripped.
triggers: syncToTextarea() — called on every state mutation (save flow step 5)

shape.providers: Array<{id, label, active, api_key, models: Array<{id, label, active, is_default, temperature, max_tokens, top_p}>}>

output_contract.consumers: Every other app on the GoUltra AI platform. Listed in docs/PROJECT.md.

failure_modes.scenario: S.data.providers is null or not an array
failure_modes.handling: Returns { providers: [], default_provider: '', default_model: '' } — consumers see empty config, which is the safe default

failure_modes.scenario: A provider with key_verified=true but enabled=false
failure_modes.handling: Excluded from export. Consumers do not see the provider until enabled is also true.

failure_modes.scenario: default_provider points at a provider that fails the inclusion rule
failure_modes.handling: default_provider is still emitted as-is; consumers must validate against their parsed providers list. Future improvement: nullify default_provider in this case.

breaking_change_warning: Any change to the output shape is a breaking change for EVERY OTHER APP on the platform. See CLAUDE.md 'What Claude will not do' list.
